emails.subject: Your [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] might be telling you something important
emails.preview: What your body is really trying to say...
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

Thank you for completing your health assessment. When we reviewed your responses, one thing stood out: [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] often signals something deeper than what shows up on standard lab work.

Most conventional approaches focus on managing individual symptoms. But when someone experiences [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] alongside the other patterns you described, it usually points to an underlying imbalance—often in hormones, gut function, or cellular energy production.

Your assessment suggests [KEY_ASSESSMENT_INSIGHT]. This is exactly the kind of root-cause issue we help people address every day at Sacramento Functional Medicine.

If you're ready to understand what's really happening and explore a personalized path forward, we'd love to talk. Dr. Godby and our team offer a free 15-minute consultation to see if we're the right fit.

[BOOKING_LINK]

Warm regards,
The Sacramento Functional Medicine Team

emails.subject: Why [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] keeps coming back
emails.preview: The biological mechanism most doctors miss
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

Think of your body like a house with a faulty electrical system. You can replace burnt-out lightbulbs all day long, but if the wiring is damaged, those bulbs will keep burning out.

[PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] works the same way. When your hormones, immune system, or digestive function fall out of balance, symptoms keep returning no matter how many times you treat them individually.

Standard medicine often looks at isolated problems: one symptom, one prescription. But functional medicine traces symptoms back to their source. In your case, the assessment pointed to [RECOMMENDED_SERVICE_OR_FOCUS]—something we see frequently and can evaluate with advanced testing.

Once we identify the underlying mechanism, we build a treatment plan that addresses the root issue, not just the surface symptoms. That's why our patients often see changes that felt impossible before.

Want to explore what's really going on? Start with a free 15-minute call.

[BOOKING_LINK]

Best,
Sacramento Functional Medicine

emails.subject: How Sarah finally resolved her [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM]
emails.preview: A patient story that might sound familiar
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

We recently worked with a patient—let's call her Sarah—who came to us after years of struggling with [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM]. She'd tried multiple approaches, seen several doctors, but nothing seemed to create lasting change.

During her evaluation, we discovered what standard labs had missed: a combination of hormone imbalance and gut dysfunction that was driving her symptoms. Once we addressed those root causes through [RECOMMENDED_SERVICE_OR_FOCUS] and personalized nutrition, Sarah started feeling like herself again within weeks.

Of course, everyone's situation is unique, and results vary. But Sarah's story is not uncommon here. When we identify and treat the actual source of symptoms—not just mask them—people often experience the kind of improvement they'd almost given up on.

If your assessment results remind you of Sarah's pattern, let's talk. Dr. Godby has been helping Sacramento patients like you for over 20 years.

[BOOKING_LINK]

With care,
Dr. Godby and Team

app_config.tagline: Optimal Health is Within Your Reach

Hormone Imbalance: Unexplained weight gain or difficulty losing weight
Chronic Fatigue: Exhaustion that doesn't improve with rest
Difficulty getting out of bed in the morning
Thyroid Issues: Cold sensitivity or always feeling cold
Gut Health: Skin issues like eczema or acne
Weight Loss: Inability to lose weight despite diet and exercise
Rapid weight gain without lifestyle changes
Emotional eating or stress-related weight gain
Menopause Symptoms: Weight gain, especially around the midsection
Low Testosterone: Loss of muscle mass or strength
Increased body fat, especially around the waist
Autoimmune Conditions: Hair loss or changes in appearance
Brain Fog: Trouble finding words or expressing thoughts
Sleep Problems: Waking too early and unable to return to sleep
Racing thoughts or anxiety at bedtime
